nodes.text: [mood:lunch]Kai brought the soup to the small kitchen table at six-twenty-something. He had warmed the soup, cut the bread into slices thick enough to dip, found four bowls and four spoons in the second drawer down, and set the table the way somebody sets a table who has been doing it for somebody else his whole life. Lena watched him do it. She had not seen Kai cook for a group in maybe two years. He had come up through his mother's kitchen and his grandmother's kitchen and his cousin Devon's kitchen and was, in a way that did not surface most weeks, very good at the ordinary work of feeding people.

They moved to the table. The four chairs were not a matched set. Lena had bought three of them at Margit's at three different points and had inherited the fourth from the previous tenant when she had moved in. The table was a small round oak from the seventies that had a wobble in one leg she had been meaning to shim for a year.
[shot:establish]Tem sat across from Lena. Kai sat on Lena's right. Finn sat on her left. The wobble in the table met Tem's elbow. Tem put a hand on the table to steady it and looked at Lena.
"I have a shim at the cabin. I'll bring one next time."

They ate in the way four people who had not eaten in a day eat — without talking, without slowing, with the small mechanical attention that hunger pulls out of a body. Lena finished a bowl and got up and got herself a second. Kai finished two slices of bread and reached for a third. Finn ate one bowl slowly. Tem ate half of one and stopped and held the spoon over the bowl for a count and set the spoon down.
"I have to say something," Tem said.
[shot:closeup tem~]The three of them looked at her.

"I want to say it now while we have eaten and before we go any further. I have been sitting with it since this morning. I owe you all an apology and I want to give it before we figure out what we do next."
[shot:insert bowl]She pushed the bowl an inch away from her.
